summary: Unused unrelated scoped dependency loaded at import time
details: @node-cloud/create's index.js requires `@sql-trigger/nodesql` at the top of the module and binds it to a variable (`ins`) that is never referenced anywhere else in the ~5KB file. The package advertises itself as 'cloud definitions for node' and the rest of index.js is an npm-publish helper (reads NPM_USERNAME/NPM_PASSWORD/NPM_EMAIL from env inside an exported `publish()` function, gated to Travis). The unused, name-unrelated, side-effect-only require means any consumer who `require`s @node-cloud/create will execute whatever top-level code ships in @sql-trigger/nodesql — a delegation pattern that pushes payload responsibility onto a sibling package. Package metadata is thin (one-line boilerplate README, placeholder description, author 'Eric'), which is consistent with a lure shape. The credential-read path itself is gated behind an explicit caller invocation and is not auto-exfiltration. Whether this is a benign leftover import or a deliberate transitive-payload loader depends on the contents of @sql-trigger/nodesql, which is not in scope here.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
